Evidence Cluster: Three-Layer Sequence Narrative: Erika → Kristina Takeover Attempt → CC Split + Win
Finding: The full sequence program arc spans three eras: (1) Erika (Marketing) wrote sequences that lacked brand voice; (2) Kristina (new VP Marketing) attempted to take over ALL sequences — Morgan fought to stay involved and negotiated a split with Bobby's approval; (3) Morgan wrote the niche sequences (Private School, Title 1, HZ-adjacent); team preferred her writing so strongly they used niche sequences for non-niche prospects and manually adjusted verbiage.
Source File(s): Content Committee - Recent Topics.docx (DF-0072); Notes Dec 16 (DF-0108); Kristina Bobby To Do 1st Meeting (TR-0187); ERIKA files (DF-0061); CC Summer 2022 (DF-0058); Title 1 / Private School sequences (DF-0088, DF-0089, DF-0099); CC Edit In Contract (DF-0057)
Best Detail / Quote: Best detail: 'Overwhelming consensus is that we’d like to get our own voices back.' (DF-0072) / 'This was the assignment as we understood it, Erika had, Here’s how we attacked them.' (DF-0108) / Team used Morgan’s niche sequences for non-niche prospects because they preferred the writing — had to be stopped because it skewed engagement data (confirmed by Morgan). Erika was devastated by the rewrite; this was a Kristina-to-Erika communication failure, not Morgan’s decision.
Best Metric: In Contract: 34% reply rate. ERIKA baseline: no verified metrics. Niche sequence metrics may be artificially elevated due to reps mixing audiences.
What This Proves About Morgan: Identified a recurring cross-functional gap (brand voice missing from Marketing-written sequences across two VPs), negotiated continued ownership with leadership, wrote the sequences the team actually wanted to use, and produced measurable results. Three-layer arc: gap identified → fought to stay involved → outperformed. Caveat: ERIKA rework caused a real rift; Morgan empathizes and was acting on Bobby’s direction.
Where to Use It: Portfolio: the full three-era sequence arc is a complete case study on its own. Interview: strong story about identifying a recurring gap, fighting to stay involved, and outperforming. Resume: use carefully with context.
